description:
Software Engineer (Backend)

Adelaide | Full-time | $85,000 - $110,000 + super + ESOP




About Us

At OptiGrid, we're building the technology to significantly improve the economics of grid-connected batteries and accelerate the clean energy transition. Our proprietary market forecasting and battery optimisation platform outperforms the status quo, with strong customer traction in a short span of time.

Backed by prominent deep-tech and clean-tech investors, our team of software engineers, data scientists, and power system modellers is growing. We're looking for a Software Engineer to help us scale our platform and API layer.




About the Role

This is a hands-on opportunity to grow quickly in a high-performing climate-tech team, shipping production code that has direct impact in one of the most important and fastest-growing markets. You'll work closely with senior engineers and domain experts from day one.




What you'll do

Build and maintain APIs (internal and external) in Python to power our forecasting, optimisation, and battery operations workflows

Deliver features end-to-end, from requirements through to production deployment, alongside senior engineers and domain experts

Write clean, well-tested code and improve API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)

Contribute to our AWS-based platform using modern DevOps practices (Git, CI/CD, Docker)

What you'll bring

A collaborative, curious mindset with a habit of asking good questions and iterating quickly

Real ownership and a high standard of quality in everything you deliver

Experience building data pipelines, delivering productionised code, or maintaining live applications (including strong internships or projects)

Python fundamentals and comfort working with time series data or databases

Working knowledge of Git; willingness to learn our CI/CD, Docker, and AWS tooling

Nice to have

Experience with async Python, typed models (Pydantic), or REST API integration

Exposure to AWS serverless or infrastructure-as-code (e.g. Terraform)

Interest in energy markets, forecasting, or optimisation




What's on offer

$85,000 - $110,000 + superannuation + Employee Share Options Plan (ESOP)

Flexible working arrangements

Fast-paced, mission-driven environment where your code has direct real-world impact

Work alongside a curious, ambitious, and tight-knit team solving hard problems

Clear growth path with mentorship and increasing ownership




How to apply

Apply via Seek with your CV and a brief cover letter explaining why you're a great fit for this role.

If you have any questions about the role, feel free to reach out for a confidential chat before applying.
